items.summary: Add a backend query to retrieve all CallbackRequest records. Add a backend query to retrieve all CarListing records that have bookingStatus set to 'booked' (i.e., all booking records with receipt details). Ensure the existing getAllListings and getCallbackRequests functions (or equivalent) are accessible for the admin dashboard. No new data models are needed — use existing CarListing and CallbackRequest types.

Create a new Admin Dashboard page at route '/admin' in the frontend. The page must have three tabbed sections: (1) 'Callback Requests' — displays a table of all customer callback requests with columns: customer name, phone number (unmasked for admin), listing ID, preferred time slot, and date/time submitted; (2) 'Bookings & Payments' — displays a table of all booked car listings with columns: car (make, model, year), registration number, seller name, booking status, receipt file name, and booked-at timestamp; (3) 'All Listings / Purchase Inquiries' — displays a table of all car listings with columns: ID, make, model, year, city, asking price, status (available/sold/booked), and posted date. Each table must be sortable by date (newest first) and include a total count at the top of each tab. Style the dashboard consistently with the AutoHub red-orange and dark-grey theme.

Add a visible 'Admin Dashboard' link in the site Navbar (desktop and mobile menu) pointing to '/admin'. The link should be labeled 'Admin' and placed at the far right of the desktop nav or at the bottom of the mobile menu. Style it as a subtle secondary link (not the primary CTA button) consistent with the existing navbar theme.
